title: 23.One Man's Meat is Another Man's Poison
filename: 23.One Man's Meat is Another Man's Poison

lines.english: Lesson 23
lines.chinese: 第23课
lines.raw: Lesson 23 | 第23课

lines.english: One man's meat is another man's poison
lines.chinese: 各有所爱
lines.raw: One man's meat is another man's poison | 各有所爱

lines.english: What was it about snails that made the writer collect them for his friend on that day in particular?
lines.chinese: 是什么让作者在那天为他的朋友特别收集蜗牛呢？
lines.raw: What was it about snails that made the writer collect them for his friend on that day in particular? | 是什么让作者在那天为他的朋友特别收集蜗牛呢？

lines.english: People become quite illogical when they try to decide what can be eaten and what cannot be eaten.
lines.chinese: 人们在决定什么能吃，什么不能吃的时候，会变得相当不合逻辑。
lines.raw: People become quite illogical when they try to decide what can be eaten and what cannot be eaten. | 人们在决定什么能吃，什么不能吃的时候，会变得相当不合逻辑。

lines.english: If you lived in the Mediterranean, for instance, you would consider octopus a great delicacy.
lines.chinese: 例如，如果你住在地中海地区，你会认为章鱼是人间美味。
lines.raw: If you lived in the Mediterranean, for instance, you would consider octopus a great delicacy. | 例如，如果你住在地中海地区，你会认为章鱼是人间美味。

lines.english: You would not be able to understand why some people find it repulsive.
lines.chinese: 你会不理解为什么有些人觉得它令人厌恶。
lines.raw: You would not be able to understand why some people find it repulsive. | 你会不理解为什么有些人觉得它令人厌恶。

lines.english: On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat-the normally accepted practice in many northern countries.
lines.chinese: 另一方面，一想到用动物脂肪炸土豆——这在许多北方国家是普遍接受的做法——你就会反胃。
lines.raw: On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat-the normally accepted practice in many northern countries. | 另一方面，一想到用动物脂肪炸土豆——这在许多北方国家是普遍接受的做法——你就会反胃。

lines.english: The sad truth is that most of us have been brought up to eat certain foods and we stick to them all our lives.
lines.chinese: 可悲的事实是，我们大多数人从小就被教导吃某些食物，并且我们一生都坚持这些食物。
lines.raw: The sad truth is that most of us have been brought up to eat certain foods and we stick to them all our lives. | 可悲的事实是，我们大多数人从小就被教导吃某些食物，并且我们一生都坚持这些食物。

lines.english: No creature has received more praise and abuse than the common garden snail.
lines.chinese: 没有哪种生物比普通的园林蜗牛得到过更多的赞扬和辱骂。
lines.raw: No creature has received more praise and abuse than the common garden snail. | 没有哪种生物比普通的园林蜗牛得到过更多的赞扬和辱骂。

lines.english: Cooked in wine, snails are a great luxury in various parts of the world.
lines.chinese: 用酒烹制的蜗牛在世界各地都是一种高级奢侈品。
lines.raw: Cooked in wine, snails are a great luxury in various parts of the world. | 用酒烹制的蜗牛在世界各地都是一种高级奢侈品。

lines.english: There are countless people who, ever since their early years, have learned to associate snails with food.
lines.chinese: 无数人从小就学会将蜗牛与食物联系在一起。
lines.raw: There are countless people who, ever since their early years, have learned to associate snails with food. | 无数人从小就学会将蜗牛与食物联系在一起。

lines.english: My friend, Robert, lives in a country where snails are despised.
lines.chinese: 我的朋友罗伯特住在一个厌恶蜗牛的国家。
lines.raw: My friend, Robert, lives in a country where snails are despised. | 我的朋友罗伯特住在一个厌恶蜗牛的国家。

lines.english: As his flat is in a large town, he has no garden of his own.
lines.chinese: 因为他的公寓在一个大城镇里，他没有自己的花园。
lines.raw: As his flat is in a large town, he has no garden of his own. | 因为他的公寓在一个大城镇里，他没有自己的花园。

lines.english: For years he has been asking me to collect snails from my garden and take them to him.
lines.chinese: 多年来他一直让我从我的花园里收集蜗牛带给他。
lines.raw: For years he has been asking me to collect snails from my garden and take them to him. | 多年来他一直让我从我的花园里收集蜗牛带给他。

lines.english: The idea never appealed to me very much, but one day, after a heavy shower,
lines.chinese: 这个想法从来没有太吸引我，但有一天，在一场大雨之后，
lines.raw: The idea never appealed to me very much, but one day, after a heavy shower, | 这个想法从来没有太吸引我，但有一天，在一场大雨之后，

lines.english: I happened to be walking in my garden when I noticed a huge number of snails taking a stroll on some of my prize plants.
lines.chinese: 我碰巧在我的花园里散步，这时我注意到大量的蜗牛在我的几株珍贵植物上爬行。
lines.raw: I happened to be walking in my garden when I noticed a huge number of snails taking a stroll on some of my prize plants. | 我碰巧在我的花园里散步，这时我注意到大量的蜗牛在我的几株珍贵植物上爬行。

lines.english: Acting on a sudden impulse, I collected several dozen, put them in a paper bag, and took them to Robert.
lines.chinese: 我一时冲动，收集了几十只，把它们放进一个纸袋里，然后带给了罗伯特。
lines.raw: Acting on a sudden impulse, I collected several dozen, put them in a paper bag, and took them to Robert. | 我一时冲动，收集了几十只，把它们放进一个纸袋里，然后带给了罗伯特。

lines.english: Robert was delighted to see me and equally pleased with my little gift.
lines.chinese: 罗伯特很高兴见到我，也同样喜欢我的小礼物。
lines.raw: Robert was delighted to see me and equally pleased with my little gift. | 罗伯特很高兴见到我，也同样喜欢我的小礼物。

lines.english: I left the bag in the hall and Robert and I went into the living room where we talked for a couple of hours.
lines.chinese: 我把袋子留在门厅里，罗伯特和我走进客厅，我们在那里聊了几个小时。
lines.raw: I left the bag in the hall and Robert and I went into the living room where we talked for a couple of hours. | 我把袋子留在门厅里，罗伯特和我走进客厅，我们在那里聊了几个小时。

lines.english: I had forgotten all about the snails when Robert suddenly said that I must stay to dinner.
lines.chinese: 我已经完全忘记了蜗牛，这时罗伯特突然说我必须留下来吃晚饭。
lines.raw: I had forgotten all about the snails when Robert suddenly said that I must stay to dinner. | 我已经完全忘记了蜗牛，这时罗伯特突然说我必须留下来吃晚饭。

lines.english: Snails would, of course, be the main dish.
lines.chinese: 蜗牛当然是主菜。
lines.raw: Snails would, of course, be the main dish. | 蜗牛当然是主菜。

lines.english: I did not fancy the idea and I reluctantly followed Robert out of the room.
lines.chinese: 我不喜欢这个主意，我只好不情愿地跟着罗伯特离开了房间。
lines.raw: I did not fancy the idea and I reluctantly followed Robert out of the room. | 我不喜欢这个主意，我只好不情愿地跟着罗伯特离开了房间。

lines.english: To our dismay,
lines.chinese: 令我们沮丧的是，
lines.raw: To our dismay, | 令我们沮丧的是，

lines.english: we saw that there were snails everywhere:
lines.chinese: 我们看到到处都是蜗牛：
lines.raw: we saw that there were snails everywhere: | 我们看到到处都是蜗牛：

lines.english: they had escaped from the paper bag
lines.chinese: 它们已经从纸袋里跑了出来
lines.raw: they had escaped from the paper bag | 它们已经从纸袋里跑了出来

lines.english: and had taken complete possession of the hall! I have never be able to look at a snail since then.
lines.chinese: 并且完全占据了门厅！从那时起，我再也无法正眼看蜗牛了。
lines.raw: and had taken complete possession of the hall! I have never be able to look at a snail since then. | 并且完全占据了门厅！从那时起，我再也无法正眼看蜗牛了。
